title: platform work catalog regeneration
purpose: D4.5 defines platform work catalog regeneration for the agent-driven expert system. Platform evolution — promotion ladder, parallel queue, reuse.
scope: Owns `D4.5` only; siblings under `D4` must not duplicate this spec.
Aligns with minimal HITL: H1 plan, H2 blocker, H3 sign-off ([INTRO-1.2](INTRO-1.2-human-touchpoint-contract-h1-h2-h3.md)).
Conflicts resolve in favor of [Vision §6 — Branch D — Platform evolution plane (parallel queue)](../../full-automation-vision-and-hierarchy.md#6-branch-d-platform-evolution-plane-parallel-queue).
behavior_steps: After new playbooks, scripts, skills, or pack fragments land, platform work runs S0 catalog regeneration when scripts exist; otherwise an economy worker with `allowed_reads` limited to new artifact paths only.
The worker updates `docs/playbooks/INDEX.md`, manifest catalog nodes, and operator discovery rows so [B4.3](B4.3-compose-first-catalog-before-improvise.md) compose-first search finds fresh artifacts.
Every INDEX row must resolve to a real file on disk—regeneration without artifact creation is invalid and fails [D6.1](D6.1-platform-done-catalog-index-row.md).
On verify pass, the conductor dequeues the promotion item; librarian and implement workers on subsequent product turns see updated catalog paths.
If INDEX rows are stale, broken, or point at missing files, pursuit blocks downstream compose-first routing at H2 until regeneration completes.
edge_cases: Operator closes laptop mid-loop â€” state.json must resume from last good dual-write.
Concurrent manual edit to queue JSON â€” conductor reloads queue each wake; last writer wins with journal note.
Platform queue depth 0 but product blocked on missing playbook â€” D3.3 priority cut skips platform drain.
Edge case `D4.5` variant 4: verify state dual-write before continuing pursuit.
Pass 3: add regression test or evidence path specific to `D4.5`.
Pass 3: cross-link related nodes in same branch index.
failure_modes: **Silent stop:** Agent ends turn without updating queue â†’ mitigated by /loop + check-hierarchy-queue.py EMPTY gate.
**False complete:** Item marked done without artifact â†’ audit-hierarchy-depth.py re-enqueues deepen pass.
**Scope bleed:** Worker edits journal/state during planning-only expansion â†’ forbidden in vision-expansion-prompt.
**Stale design:** Upstream vision Â§ changes â†’ reconcile-stale adds deepen items for affected ids.
implementation: Scheduler in autopilot workflow: `(steps_total % K) == 0` â†’ platform turn.
playbook-keeper + script extraction skills dequeue promotion items.
Validate `D4.5` against SEC-15 release checklist and parent index links.
Document `D4.5` in parent index with verify command and release tag.
Add checklist row in SEC-15 release doc for `D4.5`.
acceptance: [ ] Named script, skill, or test path exists or is listed in SEC-15 release row
narrative: D4.5 defines platform work catalog regeneration for the agent-driven expert system. Platform evolution — promotion ladder, parallel queue, reuse. In practice, the system after new playbooks, scripts, skills, or pack fragments land, platform work runs s0 catalog regeneration when scripts exist; otherwise an economy worker with `allowed_reads` limited to new artifact paths only, then the worker updates `docs/playbooks/index.md`, manifest catalog nodes, and operator discovery rows so [b4.3](b4.3-compose-first-catalog-before-improvise.md) compose-first search finds fresh artifacts.
reader_narrative:
<!-- prose-source: agent plane-d 2026-06-28 -->

Catalog and INDEX regeneration is platform work after new playbooks, scripts, skills, or pack fragments land: update `docs/playbooks/INDEX.md`, manifest catalog nodes, and operator discovery rows so compose-first search finds fresh artifacts ([B4.3](B4.3-compose-first-catalog-before-improvise.md)).

Regeneration is S0 where scripts exist; otherwise economy worker with allowed_reads on new artifact paths only. Regeneration without artifact creation is invalid—INDEX must point to real files. Librarian and implement workers depend on fresh INDEX rows; skipping regeneration breaks [B4.3](B4.3-compose-first-catalog-before-improvise.md) and fails [D6.1](D6.1-platform-done-catalog-index-row.md).
